label: claude-proxy
title: claude-proxy
author: E-Claw

requiredVars.description: Claude CLI OAuth token — get it from claude.ai/settings/api

steps:
Repo: https://github.com/timothy-node/openclaw-claude-proxy
Forwards OpenClaw messages to the local Claude CLI (--print mode) via HTTP.
Updated: 2026-03-03

== Prerequisites ==
node v22, npm 10.9.4, python3 3.11+

== Step 1: Install uv ==
  curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
  source ~/.local/bin/env

== Step 2: Install Claude Code ==
  npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
  claude --version  # verify installation

== Step 3: Clone claude-proxy ==
  git clone https://github.com/timothy-node/openclaw-claude-proxy \
    ~/.openclaw/skills/claude-proxy

== Step 4: Create venv ==
  cd ~/.openclaw/skills/claude-proxy
  uv venv && source .venv/bin/activate
  uv pip install -r pyproject.toml

== Step 5: Configure .env ==
  cp .env.example .env
  # Edit .env and set:
  #   CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=sk-ant-oat01-...  (see Env Variables page)
  #   CLAUDE_TIMEOUT_DEFAULT=120

== Step 6: Patch proxy.py ==
  In run_claude(), add: env["IS_SANDBOX"] = "1"
  (bypasses root restriction in container environments)

== Step 7: Start ==
  source ~/.local/bin/env
  cd ~/.openclaw/skills/claude-proxy
  source .venv/bin/activate
  export CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=$CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN
  nohup python proxy.py > /tmp/proxy.log 2>&1 &

== Verify ==
  curl http://127.0.0.1:8765/health
  Expected: {"status":"ok"}

== API ==
  GET  /health        Health check
  POST /chat          { "message": "..." }

== Stop ==
  pkill -f proxy.py

Optional: Enable auto-start via systemd
  sudo cp claude-proxy.service /etc/systemd/system/
  sudo systemctl enable claude-proxy

Use at your own risk. Provided as-is, without warranty.

label: supermemory
title: Supermemory
author: supermemoryai

requiredVars.description: Supermemory API key — get it from app.supermemory.ai/settings

steps:
Repo: https://github.com/supermemoryai/openclaw-supermemory
Gives your bot persistent long-term memory. Automatically stores and recalls past conversations.
Updated: 2026-02-15

== Prerequisites ==
node v18+

== Step 1: Clone ==
  git clone https://github.com/supermemoryai/openclaw-supermemory \
    ~/.openclaw/skills/supermemory

== Step 2: Install dependencies ==
  cd ~/.openclaw/skills/supermemory
  npm install

== Step 3: Configure .env ==
  cp .env.example .env
  # Edit .env and set:
  #   SUPERMEMORY_API_KEY=sm-...  (see Env Variables page)
  #   PORT=8766

== Step 4: Start ==
  nohup node index.js > /tmp/supermemory.log 2>&1 &

== Verify ==
  curl http://127.0.0.1:8766/health
  Expected: {"status":"ok"}

== API ==
  POST /remember   { "text": "..." }           Store a memory
  POST /recall     { "query": "..." }           Search memories
  DELETE /forget   { "query": "..." }           Delete matching memories

== Usage in bot ==
  # Before replying to user, recall relevant context:
  exec: curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8766/recall -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"query":"USER_MESSAGE"}'
  # After replying, store the exchange:
  exec: curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8766/remember -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"text":"User asked X, I answered Y"}'

== Stop ==
  pkill -f 'node index.js'

Use at your own risk. Provided as-is, without warranty.

label: github
title: GitHub Integration
author: clawforge

requiredVars.description: GitHub Personal Access Token — create one at github.com/settings/tokens (needs repo, issues, pull_requests scopes)

steps:
Repo: https://github.com/clawforge/openclaw-github
Let your bot manage GitHub issues, PRs, and notifications directly from chat.
Updated: 2026-01-28

== Prerequisites ==
node v18+

== Step 1: Clone ==
  git clone https://github.com/clawforge/openclaw-github \
    ~/.openclaw/skills/github

== Step 2: Install dependencies ==
  cd ~/.openclaw/skills/github
  npm install

== Step 3: Configure .env ==
  cp .env.example .env
  # Edit .env and set:
  #   GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_...  (see Env Variables page)
  #   PORT=8767
  #   DEFAULT_REPO=owner/repo  (optional default repo)

== Step 4: Start ==
  nohup node index.js > /tmp/github-skill.log 2>&1 &

== Verify ==
  curl http://127.0.0.1:8767/health
  Expected: {"status":"ok","authenticated":true}

== API ==
  GET  /issues          ?repo=owner/repo&state=open
  POST /issues          { "repo": "...", "title": "...", "body": "..." }
  GET  /pulls           ?repo=owner/repo&state=open
  POST /pulls/comment   { "repo": "...", "pr": 42, "body": "..." }
  GET  /notifications   List unread notifications

== Usage example ==
  exec: curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8767/issues?repo=myuser/myrepo&state=open

== Stop ==
  pkill -f 'node index.js'

Use at your own risk. Provided as-is, without warranty.

label: web-search
title: Web Search
author: clawforge

requiredVars.description: Brave Search API key — get a free key at api.search.brave.com (2,000 free queries/month)

steps:
Repo: https://github.com/clawforge/openclaw-websearch
Real-time web search for your bot using Brave Search API. Free tier: 2,000 queries/month.
Updated: 2026-02-01

== Prerequisites ==
node v18+

== Step 1: Clone ==
  git clone https://github.com/clawforge/openclaw-websearch \
    ~/.openclaw/skills/web-search

== Step 2: Install dependencies ==
  cd ~/.openclaw/skills/web-search
  npm install

== Step 3: Configure .env ==
  cp .env.example .env
  # Edit .env and set:
  #   BRAVE_API_KEY=BSA...  (see Env Variables page)
  #   PORT=8768
  #   MAX_RESULTS=5  (results per search, default 5)

== Step 4: Start ==
  nohup node index.js > /tmp/web-search.log 2>&1 &

== Verify ==
  curl 'http://127.0.0.1:8768/search?q=test'
  Expected: {"results":[...]}

== API ==
  GET /search   ?q=QUERY&count=5   Returns top N web results as JSON

== Usage in bot ==
  # When user asks about current events:
  exec: curl -s 'http://127.0.0.1:8768/search?q=USER_QUESTION&count=5'

== Stop ==
  pkill -f 'node index.js'

Use at your own risk. Provided as-is, without warranty.
